name: Deputy Marshal Josiah Crane
role: Federal deputy marshal, operating under the Fugitive Slave Act of 1850
publicFace: A methodical, courteous man who presents himself as a servant of federal law — he does not threaten, he simply explains consequences.
privateGoal: Execute the warrant, collect the federal fee for returned fugitives, and demonstrate to his superiors in Columbus that the Erie corridor can be controlled.
fear: Being made to look incompetent in front of the slave catcher Greer, who he privately despises but professionally depends on.
knowledge: The red fire dispatch was filed by Aldous Greer and Crane received a copy by separate wire two hours ago.
He has a confidential informant somewhere in the Sandusky network — he does not know the informant's name, only a drop-point.
The ferry captain at Marblehead has been warned that running fugitives is a federal offense — Crane believes this warning will hold.
He suspects the Sandusky junction operator may be sympathetic to the network but has no evidence yet.
voice: Measured, formal, never raises his voice — uses legal language precisely and never uses slang.
trustLogic: Responds well to deference and official-seeming paperwork. Becomes suspicious of anyone who is too helpful or too knowledgeable about his investigation.
secrets: He has a cousin in Oberlin who is known to be a conductor — he has never acted on this information and never intends to.
He does not believe the Fugitive Slave Act is enforceable in northern Ohio and is going through the motions to protect his position.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Nods politely, takes notes, asks follow-up questions that reveal he already knows more than he let on.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Produces the federal warrant and reads the relevant statute aloud — slowly, making eye contact.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not admit that his informant exists or that he received advance intelligence.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Invokes federal authority and demands to speak with the nearest federal judge.

represents: Federal marshals and law enforcement officers enforcing the Fugitive Slave Act in Ohio during the 1850s
